scenario_a.label: Russian reparations obligation (acknowledged — pending settlement)
scenario_a.source_label: RDNA3 $486B claim; ICJ Ukraine v. Russia (2022); UN GA Res. ES-11/5
scenario_a.note: Ukraine's documented war damage claim is $486B (RDNA3, Feb 2024). Full economic loss including GDP and future damage may reach $2 trillion. Russia's reparations obligation is legally acknowledged under international law. Transfer timeline contingent on peace settlement; can be securitised against future award to back Trust corpus now.

scenario_a.label: Frozen Russian sovereign assets — ERA windfall interest
scenario_a.source_label: EU Council Reg. 2024/1469; G7 Vilnius communiqué 2023
scenario_a.note: ~$280B frozen in G7/EU custodians. Principal locked pending legal resolution; ~$3B/yr interest stream backs the ERA loan.

scenario_a.label: ERA extraordinary-revenue acceleration loan
scenario_a.note: 50-year loan collateralised against ERA windfall interest. First $50B tranche disbursed 2024.

scenario_a.label: EU Ukraine Facility — trust-eligible tranche
scenario_a.note: €50B EU Facility (2024–27). Trust-eligible reconstruction tranche contingent on reform milestones.

scenario_a.label: Bilateral donor pledges (US, UK, DE, FR, JP, CA)
scenario_a.source_label: G7 Hiroshima / Vilnius pledges; Ukraine donor conference
scenario_a.note: Confirmed bilateral pledges from G7 members for reconstruction, excludes military aid.

scenario_a.label: Diaspora war bonds (UA sovereign)
scenario_a.source_label: UA Ministry of Finance; diaspora capital survey 2023
scenario_a.note: Ukrainian sovereign bonds targeted at 8M+ diaspora. Conservative estimate based on 2022–23 uptake rates.

scenario_a.label: Privatisation proceeds (SOE sales)
scenario_a.note: State-owned enterprise privatisation during wartime. Low baseline — investor appetite constrained by conflict risk.

scenario_b.label: ICJ / Hague tribunal reparations — direct transfer from Russia
scenario_b.source_label: ICJ Ukraine v. Russia (2022); UN GA Res. ES-11/5; RDNA3 $486B claim
scenario_b.note: Post-war reparations awarded by ICJ or negotiated peace treaty. Ukraine's documented direct damage claim is $486B (RDNA3, Feb 2024). Full economic loss including GDP and future damage may reach $2 trillion. Range reflects negotiated settlement discount.

scenario_b.label: Full transfer of frozen Russian sovereign assets (~$280B principal)
scenario_b.source_label: REPO Act (US, 2024); G7 frozen-asset transfer framework; EU Council
scenario_b.note: Full $280B principal transfer once G7 legal frameworks permit post-ceasefire. Requires US REPO Act execution and EU unanimity.

scenario_b.label: EU reconstruction facility — post-2027 successor programme
scenario_b.source_label: EU Ukraine Facility post-2027 projection; EC recovery roadmap
scenario_b.note: Successor programme to the 2024–27 EU Ukraine Facility. Scale contingent on ceasefire and accession trajectory.

scenario_b.label: Post-war bilateral reconstruction pledges
scenario_b.source_label: IMF / World Bank Lugano reconstruction conference projections
scenario_b.note: Expanded bilateral pledges post-ceasefire. Includes Marshall Plan-style conditioned programmes.

scenario_b.label: Diaspora reconstruction bonds (10-year programme)
scenario_b.source_label: UA diaspora capital survey; post-war return projections
scenario_b.note: Long-term diaspora bond programme backed by reconstruction revenue streams and sovereign guarantee.

scenario_b.label: Post-war privatisation and asset monetisation
scenario_b.note: Large-scale SOE privatisation and land monetisation post-ceasefire. EBRD estimates $15–50B range depending on investor sentiment.
